accepted.reason: Random graph edge-generation loops are a strong fit for Cython-oriented optimization tasks.
Triangle geometry computations expose dense numeric operations suitable for speedup-focused optimization.
Prime-sieve style boolean array iteration is a canonical benchmark-friendly kernel.
Waveform peak/window scans map cleanly to loop-heavy numeric processing tasks.
Affine transforms and bounding-box passes are practical geometry kernels with optimization headroom.
Heap percolation/update logic is a useful algorithmic pattern for optimization training.

skipped.reason: Parser transport buffer wrapper is mostly integration glue and less suitable as a standalone optimization exercise.
Third-party transport wrapper code is I/O integration focused rather than core numeric or algorithmic compute.
Cython language-behavior test fixture is not a representative optimization target.
State-space traversal depends on project-specific runtime context and offers low transfer as a standalone example.
